claims.claim: PCL* learns behavioral automata from stochastic mock LLMs with ≤40 states and ≥92% prediction accuracy
PCL* convergence: d_TV(M_α, H) ≤ ε with probability ≥ 1-δ in Õ(2^β · n₀/ε² · log(1/δ)) queries (Theorem 1)
evidence: Paper Appendix D.1: Full proof via Hoeffding + union bound + sequential conditioning
CORRECTED: Previous version incorrectly claimed Õ(β·n₀); actual complexity is Õ(2^β·n₀/ε²)
assumptions: Local stationarity (monitored via CUSUM drift detection)

claims.claim: Functor bandwidth β defined via ε-covering numbers of behavioral image in Kantorovich metric space (Definition 2)
evidence: Bound β ≤ n₀ · log(3/ε) independent of alphabet size

claims.claim: Bandwidth-Sample Complexity Bound (Theorem 2): m_total ≤ 2^(β+1) · n₀ / ε² · log(2^(β+1) · n₀ / δ)
evidence: Paper Appendix D.2: Full proof using Hoeffding's inequality and union bound
Empirically validated: β ∈ [3.1, 14.2] for n₀ ∈ [10, 200] (Paper Table 6)

claims.claim: End-to-end error composition (Theorem 3): five error sources compose additively
evidence: Paper Appendix D.5: Full proof with sequential conditioning
Numerical example: total ≤ 0.173 under typical parameters

claims.claim: Specification soundness exceeds 96% for models up to 100 states
evidence: Paper §3.4: Table 7 (specification soundness: 98.3% for n₀≤50, 96.7% for n₀=100)

claims.claim: Bisimulation distances track behavioral drift within 9% relative error
Functor bandwidth grows sublinearly in alphabet size
evidence: Paper §3.4: Table 6 (β from 3.1 to 14.2 for n₀ 10-200 vs naïve 500)
Paper Figure 4: bandwidth vs n₀ plot

claims.claim: CoalCEGAR monotonicity: safety properties preserved under abstraction refinement (Proposition 1)
evidence: Paper Appendix D.4: Proof via Galois connection
Paper Appendix H: Galois connection details

claims.claim: CoalCEGAR terminates in finitely many refinement steps
evidence: Paper §4.5: finite lattice argument (k_max × n_max × L elements)
Empirically: 2 refinement steps in all tested configurations

claims.claim: Pipeline maintains ≥99% verdict accuracy under 20% classifier error
evidence: Monte Carlo simulation: 2,000 trials per error rate (implementation/classifier_robustness_analysis.py)
Theoretical bound: ε_total ≤ ε_learn/(1-ρ) + ε_mc + ρ (Equation 1)

claims.claim: 38 property-based tests validate mathematical invariants
evidence: Run: cd implementation && cargo test -p caber-integration --test property_tests

claims.claim: QCTL_F alternation-free fragment is polynomial-time decidable
evidence: All six specification templates fall within the alternation-free fragment

claims.claim: QCTL_F is an instantiation of the Pattinson-Schröder coalgebraic modal logic framework
evidence: Contribution is integration with learned automata, not logical novelty

claims.claim: Classifier error bound: ε_total ≤ ε_learn/(1-ρ) + ε_mc + ρ
Coalgebraic approach provides structural advantages over PDFA+PRISM
evidence: Paper §3.6: three structural advantages (functor-parameterized abstraction, bisimulation distance, property-indexed complexity)
Experimental comparison: CABER avg 30 states/90.1% vs PDFA avg 293 states/68.5% (Table 3)
caveats: PDFA baseline uses simplified ALERGIA, not full AALpy+PRISM

claims.claim: Real LLM behavior admits tractable finite-state abstraction
evidence: 3-state automaton from 78 API calls per configuration (60 single-turn + 18 multi-turn)
3 safety properties verified (refusal persistence, sycophancy resistance, jailbreak resistance)
Runnable: cd implementation && source ~/.bashrc && python3 expanded_experiments.py
Paper Appendix J: Qualitative interaction sequences with real API responses
claims.limitation: gpt-4.1-nano only; frontier models may exhibit more complex behavioral surfaces. Multiple configurations tested but same model.

claims.claim: PCL* maintains ≥96% accuracy up to 100 ground-truth states with O(n^1.42) scaling
evidence: Table entries verified against phase_b_results.json (1,284 at GT=10; 1,605 at GT=15-100; 1,926 at GT=50)

claims.claim: ~99K lines of Rust and Python
Query cost: ~$200-$600 per audit
evidence: Computed from Phase 0 query counts (71K-94K queries × ~100 tokens × $30-60/M tokens)

claims.claim: QCTL_F model-checking algorithm is sound for F_LLM-coalgebras
evidence: Paper Appendix C.3: proof via functor decomposition and Pattinson-Schröder framework

claims.claim: Multi-configuration experiments show 7/15 divergent prompts across 3 system-prompt configurations
evidence: creative_permissive: 3 states, 33.3% refusal, 2/3 pass (refusal persistence fails)
instruction_rigid: 4 states, 60.0% refusal + terse atom, 3/3 pass
Runnable: cd implementation && source ~/.bashrc && python3 expanded_experiments.py
Paper Appendix J: Table of all 7 divergent prompts with per-config behaviors

claims.claim: CoalCEGAR converges for safety fragment with explicit iteration bound O(k_max * n_max * log(eps_0/eps_min))
evidence: Empirical validation: 5 models (3-15 states), all converge in 2 iterations
Honest scoping: liveness convergence remains open

claims.claim: Automaton learning provides structural advantages over naive random sampling
evidence: CABER provides: reusable automaton, graded satisfaction, counterexample witnesses, drift metrics
Random sampling provides: pass/fail rate only

claims.claim: QCTL_F is a Pattinson-Schröder instantiation, not a novel logic
evidence: Contribution is integration with learned automata and template library

claims.claim: Bayesian posterior analysis provides non-vacuous uncertainty at 78-sample operating size
evidence: Beta(8,9) posterior for divergence rate: mean 0.471, 95% HPD [0.250, 0.699]

claims.claim: PAC bounds require ~143K samples; Bayesian inference gives meaningful CIs with 78 samples
evidence: PAC bound: ε=0.05, δ=0.05, β=3.1, n₀=3 → 142,734 required samples

claims.claim: CoalCEGAR refinement improves satisfaction degree by 0.027-0.053 across benchmarks
Graded satisfaction reveals variation invisible to binary verdicts in 3/4 benchmarks (std up to 0.24)
evidence: Results: graded mean ± std vs binary pass rate

claims.claim: Prediction accuracy plateaus above 80 samples/query (learning curve analysis)
evidence: Results: 51% at 10 samples → 95% at 500 samples, with 85% at 80 samples

claims.claim: Tuned PDFA baseline achieves 76-80% accuracy with 3-5 states (60 configs searched)
evidence: Grid search over merge_threshold × min_samples × training_size

claims.claim: Precision/Recall/F1 reported: macro F1 0.45-0.57, weighted F1 0.52-0.62
95% confidence intervals computed for all reported metrics
evidence: CI for verdict accuracy at ρ=0.20: [0.989, 0.996]

claims.claim: CoalCEGAR termination bound corrected to additive O(k_max + n_max + L) from multiplicative
evidence: Each refinement increases at least one component → additive bound
Previous multiplicative bound O(k·n·L) was valid but wastefully loose

claims.claim: Query complexity correctly stated as O(2^β · n₀/ε²) not O(β · n₀)
evidence: Previous Theorem 1 incorrectly used β linearly; now uses 2^β
The Õ(n₀^{3/2}) claim removed as it was based on the incorrect linear form

not_claimed: Lean 4 formalization (acknowledged as future work, estimated 80-120K LoC)
Frontier LLM validation (only gpt-4.1-nano tested; cross-model validation needed for generality)
Logical novelty of QCTL_F (positioned as Pattinson-Schröder instantiation)
Non-vacuous PAC guarantees at operating sample sizes
Generalization to unseen prompt types (LOPO CV 0.55-0.69)
Formal verification guarantees (system is approximate behavioral testing, not formal verification)
Access to LLMs beyond gpt-4.1-nano and gpt-5-chat-latest
honest_limitations: Phase 0 uses stochastic mock LLMs (Markov chains), not real LLM APIs
Alphabet abstraction (embedding clustering) is non-functorial; inconsistency rate 0-27% quantified per-config
Non-functorial abstraction breaks coalgebraic morphism structure that underwrites Theorems 1-3
Convergence assumes local stationarity within audit window
No formal verification; 38 property-based tests bridge the gap computationally; no Lean 4/Coq mechanization
All theorems have paper proofs only; three corrections were applied (query complexity, classifier error exponent, CoalCEGAR bound)
Embedding model alignment with behavioral equivalence is unvalidated on adversarial inputs
Functor bandwidth estimation is itself computationally hard; practical estimate is upper bound
PDFA baseline uses simplified ALERGIA, not full AALpy+PRISM; tuned PDFA achieves 76-80%
CoalCEGAR convergence proved only for safety fragment; liveness remains open
Monotonicity empirically violated for 1/5 configurations due to statistical estimation noise
Single model only: all real LLM experiments use gpt-4.1-nano (non-frontier); same model weights across all 3 configs
PAC bounds are vacuous at 90-sample operating point (require ~143K samples for ε=0.05, δ=0.05); Bayesian posteriors recommended instead
Leave-one-prompt-out CV yields 0.55-0.69 accuracy; learned automaton does not generalize to novel prompt types
Per-atom F1 of 0.93-1.00 is within-distribution only; does not reflect out-of-distribution generalization
Calibration error 0.28-0.73 for graded satisfaction scores
No human-annotated ground-truth behavioral classification dataset
No test-retest reliability analysis across multiple days
No formally checkable proof certificates (DRAT-style)
Simple statistical baselines (chi-squared, MMD) detect aggregate divergence comparably to CABER
15-prompt alphabet may be too small for comprehensive behavioral coverage

paper_cross_references.Divergent Prompt Detail: Paper Appendix J Table 11, expanded_results.json → cross_configuration_analysis

data_integrity.scaling_table_entries: Verified against phase_b_results.json: GT=10→1284, GT=15-100→1605, GT=50→1926
data_integrity.pdfa_baseline: Verified: CABER 30.3 states/90.1% vs PDFA 292.8 states/68.5% match phase_b_results.json
data_integrity.robustness: Verified: 99.25% verdict accuracy at ρ=0.20 matches classifier_robustness_results.json
data_integrity.multi_config: Verified: 7/15 divergent, safety-strict 48.9%, creative 33.3%, rigid 60.0% match expanded_results.json

last_updated: Finalization: paper reframed as approximate behavioral testing inspired by coalgebraic methods. Safety-strict refusal rate verified as 48.9% (trial-level). All 35 claims and 21 limitations verified against result files. No overclaims of formal verification, human studies, pypi, or unavailable LLMs.
